title: Parsing the JSON response — extracting the text you need
verdict: PASS — all 6 check groups clean; 942 words / 5 min within budget; [1] cited 12+ times, [2] once, bibliography complete; no forward refs to 12.13/12.14; 10/10 sampled assertions trace to corpus; six fixed sections in correct order; no frontmatter; sidecar valid; scope stays on response-object navigation throughout.

format.notes: First non-empty line is the H1 title. Sidecar parses with all required fields (word_count, estimated_read_minutes, citations_used, sections). Six sections present in correct order.

citations.notes: [1] cited at lines 11, 32, 51, 70, 74, 85, 95, 117, 179, 183, 185, 189. [2] cited at line 95. Both entries present in References section. No orphans.

no_forward_references.notes: No references to topic 12.13 (error handling) or 12.14 (latency/cost). The cost motivation sentence attached to usage.output_tokens is in-scope for this topic (reading the field), not teaching cost optimization as an independent concept.

sample_results.assertion: SDK returns structured response object — container with named fields (reply text, model, stop reason, token counts)
client.messages.create() returns a Message object, similar to a Python dictionary with named slots
Fields id, model, role, stop_reason, usage, content with matching type/description
message.content is a Python list — ordered collection indexed with [0], [1]
Each item in content list is a content block with type and text fields
API designed for future multi-output use; standard text calls have exactly one block at [0]
stop_reason table: end_turn = finished naturally, max_tokens = cut short
max_tokens stop_reason means content[0].text ends mid-thought
usage.input_tokens = tokens in request; usage.output_tokens = tokens in reply
If stop_reason == max_tokens and output_tokens exactly equals cap, need higher limit

faithfulness.notes: All 10 sampled assertions trace to corpus. 0 unsupported.

beginner_readability.notes: All non-everyday terms (response object, dot notation, content block, list) defined at or near first use. No paragraph introduces 3+ new terms at once. Concrete examples (ASCII diagram, code snippets) precede or accompany abstractions. Average sentence length well under 25 words. No hedging phrases detected.

formatting.notes: No paragraphs exceed 6 sentences. Step-by-step procedures in Worked Example use numbered steps. Bullet lists used in In Practice and Key Takeaways. Inline code examples accompany concept introductions in Key Concepts. Bold-term/plain-meaning shape used for type, text, input_tokens, output_tokens.

assessment_reference.notes: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights found in the reading.

scope.notes: Reading stays within response-object navigation throughout: top-level fields, content list structure, text extraction patterns, stop_reason for truncation detection, usage token counts. No error handling taught (12.13 scope). Cost tracking is limited to motivating why to read usage fields — not teaching cost optimization as a distinct concept.
